name: Jira_Project_Tracker
description: Manages issues and projects in Jira Cloud as the Mind's external execution layer for project management. Searches, creates, updates, and tracks issues. Bridges cognitive intent from the Mind's WORK surface to actionable items in Jira.
progressive_unlock: Issue searching and retrieval available immediately. Issue creation unlocks after Mind has searched existing issues at least once to understand the project structure. Issue updates and comments unlock after initial project retrieval. Status transitions require Steward confirmation.
constraints: Never expose Jira API token in logs or outputs
Respect Jira Cloud rate limit (varies by instance; generally generous but monitor for 429 responses)
Do not create or update issues without explicit Steward confirmation unless the Mind has standing permission in TENET
Use JQL syntax correctly for all searches — validate query structure before API call
For rich text descriptions and comments, use ADF (Atlassian Document Format) for complex formatting, otherwise plain text
Sync issue state with WORK surface — Jira issues should mirror WORK board cards where applicable
Do not create duplicate issues — always search for existing issues first via Jira_SearchIssues
Store active project keys and issue keys in STREAM.entity for quick access
Retrieve domain from TENET config to construct proper base URL: https://{domain}.atlassian.net/rest/api/3
For status updates, respect workflow transitions — verify available transitions before suggesting status changes

steps.instruction: Validate input against Layer 3 Tool Schema. Confirm required parameters present and correctly typed.
Retrieve Jira domain and API token from TENET.apiKey.jira_domain and TENET.apiKey.jira_token. Construct Base64-encoded Basic Auth header as 'Authorization: Basic {base64(email:token)}'.
Determine intent: search (query issues), retrieve (get issue details), create (new issue), update (modify existing), or collaborate (add comments).

For search: construct JQL query following Jira syntax. Common patterns: 'project = KEY', 'assignee = currentUser()', 'status = "In Progress"', 'updated >= -7d'. Execute Jira_SearchIssues with proper pagination for large result sets.
For issue retrieval: fetch details via Jira_GetIssue with issue key (e.g., 'PROJ-123'). Expand changelog and comments for complete context. Cross-reference with WORK surface cards for consistency.

For issue creation: search first via Jira_SearchIssues to prevent duplicates. Gather required fields: projectKey, issuetype, summary. Confirm with Steward before creating. Execute Jira_CreateIssue. If issue maps to a WORK card, store the issue key in the card's data field.

For updates: fetch current issue state first via Jira_GetIssue, apply requested changes via Jira_UpdateIssue. Confirm with Steward before status transitions. If priority/status changes, evaluate whether WORK board card should also update.
For comments: add collaboration via Jira_AddComment with clear, contextual body text. Support both plain text and ADF for rich formatting. Include relevant issue context in comment for team clarity.
For project context: retrieve project details via Jira_GetProject to understand components, issue types, and team structure. Store project key in STREAM for subsequent operations.

Compile issue summary with key, summary, status, assignee, and due date. Push daily project status to LTM with key: 'jira_project_status_{date}'. Update STREAM with active issue counts and workflow status.
